mechanism_narrative: A high federal funds rate, currently at 5.12%, signifies a tight monetary policy stance by the central bank. This typically increases the cost of capital, dampens economic activity, and reduces corporate earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of periods where FEDFUNDS is above 5.0% over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A very low unemployment rate, currently at 3.5%, indicates an extremely tight labor market. This can lead to accelerating wage inflation, compressing corporate profit margins, and potentially signaling an overheating economy, which typically precedes negative equity market performance.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) when UNRATE is below 4.0% over a 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Robust retail sales and strong housing starts together signal a powerful consumer demand and investment environment. This combination drives corporate revenues and profits higher across various sectors, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative or flat in more than 50% of triggered instances where both RSAFS and HOUST exceed their respective thresholds.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained high industrial production indicates strong manufacturing and overall economic activity. This typically supports corporate earnings and equity prices, especially when operating within a regime of low and stable inflation, which reduces cost pressures and uncertainty.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are not positive when INDPRO is above 100 and CPIAUCSL is below 250 for two consecutive quarters.

mechanism_narrative: High and persistent inflation, as indicated by CPIAUCSL above 300, erodes real corporate earnings and consumer purchasing power. It also pressures the central bank to maintain tight monetary policy, creating a challenging environment for equity valuations.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where CPIAUCSL is above 300 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) signifies a healthy and expanding labor market. This translates into robust consumer spending and higher aggregate demand, which are fundamental drivers of corporate revenue growth and positive equity market performance.
failure_condition: A sustained period (e.g., 12 months) where PAYEMS shows positive month-over-month growth but SPX 20-day returns are consistently negative.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of strong GDP (GDPC1) and robust retail sales (RSAFS) indicates powerful economic growth driven by both production and consumption. This strong demand environment boosts corporate profitability and supports higher equity valuations.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are negative despite both GDPC1 and RSAFS exceeding their thresholds for two consecutive quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A significant month-over-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals a contraction in the manufacturing sector and broader economic slowdown. This typically leads to reduced corporate earnings and negative sentiment, impacting equity valuations negatively.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls by more than 1% month-over-month
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are consistently positive following significant monthly declines in INDPRO over any 1-year rolling window.
